ground_truth.title: The Most Affordable Online Master's in ArtificiaI Intelligence Programs
ground_truth.main_content: It’s hard to look anywhere online and not see the mention of artificial intelligence or AI. Technology is having massive impacts on everything from healthcare to how people make art. The explosion of artificial intelligence also opens up new kinds of jobs and career options. One of the best ways to jumpstart or rapidly transition a career into artificial intelligence is to obtain a master’s degree. Here are the key points based on the 14 programs we've compiled: Lowest-Cost School: Colorado State University Global at $13,428. Average Cost: $44,107. Most Expensive School: Duke University at $98,970. Cost-Effective Advantage of Online Programs: Only a few years ago, there were hardly any standalone master's programs dedicated solely to preparing students for the unique challenges and opportunities in artificial intelligence. Today, more and more colleges and universities are quickly adapting to the surge in demand and offering new programs. Not only that, but increasingly, there are some great online options for obtaining a master's in artificial intelligence. There are several advantages to pursuing an online master's degree, and one of them is the overall cost. The rankings include Colorado State University Global ($13,428), University of the Cumberlands ($17,019), Fairfax University of America ($18,288), Maryville University ($27,225), University of San Diego ($28,950), Pennsylvania State University ($35,211), DePaul University ($38,208), Rochester Institute of Technology ($42,090), Illinois Institute of Technology ($53,400), University of Texas at Austin ($57,390), Stevens Institute of Technology ($57,900), Drexel University ($64,710), Johns Hopkins University ($64,710), and Duke University ($98,970). The page also lists AI scholarships including AFCEA STEM Majors Scholarships ($2,500), AAAI Student Scholarships, Google PhD Fellowship Program ($42,000), Great Minds in STEM Scholarship ($500-$5,000), IBM PhD Fellowship ($60,000), Joanne Pransky Celebration of Women in Robotics ($1,000), Microsoft Research PhD Fellowship ($42,000), NVIDIA Graduate Fellowship Program (up to $60,000), SWE Collegiate/Graduate Scholars ($1,000 to $3,000), and UPE/ACM Scholarship Award ($1,500).
